div.head: [Untitled]

opener.dateline:
American Legation,
Quito, January 13
; received January 15, 1912.

div.p: Confirmation received of report of Government victory at Huigra. As counter-revolution may break out at any time at Guayaquil, I have advised the Consul General and the Yorktown to refuse to receive political refugees. In my opinion asylum should under no circumstances be given to either Eloy Alfaro or Montero. I am reliably informed that the former will seek refuge on the Yorktown if the revolution fail. I urge that instructions in the above sense be sent by the Department to the Consul General and to the Yorktown, in view of bad telegraphic service from Quito.
